reviews.author: Khanh Nguyen (Khanh IVIata)
reviews.text: Du Gia Waterfall is a beautiful and relaxing stop on the Hà Giang Loop. The waterfall is not too big but very natural and peaceful, surrounded by green mountains and fresh air. You can swim, sit by the water, or just relax and enjoy the view. The water is cool and refreshing, perfect after a long day of riding. The area feels quiet and local, not too crowded. A great place to slow down, take photos, and enjoy nature in Du Gia.

reviews.author: Leo van Galen
reviews.text: Very nice waterfall with a little cliff jump too! Perfect for a sunny day, there’s a ‘life guard’ at the top with some rules about jumping in so it’s pretty safe. Came here on our final day of the loop and it was very fun. Nice to just sit and watch too if it’s too cold to get in.

reviews.author: Sabri Kürüm
reviews.text: It is a little crowded and they are playing very loud music. More like a beach club than a nature attraction. Still glad i got to see it

reviews.author: Emiel Milner
reviews.text: Empty when we went as the weather wasn’t great but normally the tours stop and gets rammo. Very nice though and short walk up. Jumping spot is a decent height too.

reviews.author: Hye Huang
reviews.text: The scenery is very natural and beautiful, you can take pictures, take a cool bath here, however, the way to get here is quite far and there is a difficult part to go, it is not close to other tourist destinations, so consider when choosing to add to your itinerary

reviews.author: Ollie Boon
reviews.text: So much fun with a decent hight jump. Usually lots of people flipping and flopping 😝 so it’s good fun. It’s a good stop if you’re on the loop.

reviews.author: Luc Diefenbach
reviews.text: Get there by motorcycle walk about five minutes and you’re there in this little secluded area with a shop where you can rent a towel if you wanna jump in and drinks and music. It’s a lively place and usually a popular stop for the loop tours to pass through. The water was freezing, but had to do it and jumped, and it was worth it. It felt amazing and it’s cool to jump off a waterfall.

reviews.author: Leona Joyfull Life
reviews.text: The waterfall is small and beautiful with clear blue water. The path to the waterfall is along a large stream with a very peaceful village scene. This is an ideal check-in location if you love nature, like swimming in clear water, or jumping from cliffs. If you like tall waterfalls, you can skip this place. As for me, I like the stream leading to Du Gia waterfall (See more in the video, pictures) #handinhandadventure

reviews.author: ANH KHOA NGUYEN
reviews.text: Stunning waterfalls, just a short and easy walk to reach. It’s a great spot for a refreshing dip. you can even jump from the cliffs into the water. There’s even a little bar with music.

reviews.author: Nia Mh
reviews.text: Beautiful waterfall. Best time to visit is midday after the Ha Giang Loop tourists are gone. It is one of the stops on the Ha Giang loop, so it gets very busy in the mornings and then again in evening. If you go during the day it’s just local kids. Pristine water and great swimming spot. More secluded swimming areas if you walk further downstream. Optional 10k donation, free parking. You can drive all the way up to entrance, cafes will try make you park at their stall.

reviews.author: Millie Lane
reviews.text: Dangerous, scary, nearly died from drowning from the under water current and had to be pulled out by 6 full grown men. I was on the ha Giang loop tour, please don’t go in there. Everyone was jumping high and was fine and I stepped in from the rock and the current was just circling there so don’t be deceived!!!!! I am a strong swimmer too.

reviews.author: Chris Caruso
reviews.text: These falls are a MUST. We came here on day 4 of our Ha Giang tour and the day was epic starting here. The drive out to here from the Du Gia town is stunning and the little area you hike to is a great atmosphere and sweet swimming hole. A few heights you can jump from and yours come and go for about 45 minutes so if you’re solo wait for the groups to cycle through or come later I. The day! Most groups will be there in the AM from Du Gia. ALSO! Don’t miss out on Du Gia! There was an insanely cool market at the beginning of the road to the falls and it had the best prices. I would’ve spent the day there had I not been in a tour. I got a great pair of riding pants for 100k!

reviews.author: Sarah Elsmore
reviews.text: GREAT WATERFALL EXPERIENCE! Highly recommend stopping at this waterfall along the Ha Giang Loop. It was easy to walk too (about 15mins from the road where you park) and it was flat ground. You can swim in the waterfall and even jump into the water. Lots of tourist when I went but if you got there early enough, it would be a lot more quiet.

reviews.author: cameron holmes
reviews.text: It’s a cool waterfall! Very busy, if you’re brave enough you can jump from the top. It’s a nice place for a stop off and a dip on a warm day. There’s also a cafe there for a snack or some drinks. Lively but a cool place. Toilets are available for a fee. I can’t remember if there’s and entrance fee.

reviews.author: Robotllamas
reviews.text: This waterfall is absolutely beautiful and the scenery is picturesque! Definitely worth a visit for the views. The only things to be wary of are how slippy the rocks around the pool are as me and my partner both slipped in and the rocks are quite sharp, the other thing is the current in the pool is very strong and if you're not a great swimmer it can be scary

reviews.author: Thomas Munnichs
reviews.text: In the middle of the epic mountains of North Vietnam, there is a place you can visit. This waterfall. You know, mountains, those giants of nature, millions of years they have been here. Their surrounding silence fills you with gratitude and amazement for the natural world. The small villages in these mountains are an authentic view way into a past that’s centuries ago in the west. Then you have this waterfall, where they play loud music and scream drunk. Get these people a one way ticket home. It’s locals who facilitate this, so don’t talk about tourism destroying your country in 5 years if you have places like this.

reviews.author: Mikey P
reviews.text: Honestly quite a nice waterfall, clear water, nice temp, can swim, however we went in the morning and it was packed easy rider groups with loud music blasting, they all seemed to leave by 11:30am. If solo maybe better at other periods of the day Can park at any number of the vendors then walk an easy 200m path to the waterfall and small pools.

reviews.author: Marco Andrade
reviews.text: I'm not the biggest fan of waterfalls, this one was no different. You will find plenty of people, locals and tourists. The highlight for me was getting there. Outstanding views. Ridiculously stunning.

reviews.author: Megaworld Asia
reviews.text: This is a very scenic location, and a great SWIMMING SPOT, near Du Gia Village. The best way of getting there is to use GOOGLE MAPS GPS to get to PARKING AREA. The FEE for motorbike parking is 20,000 VND. From the parking area it's then a 200 meter hike, along a dirt trail, to WATERFALL # 1. Note: If you want to beat the crowds (tour groups) ARRIVE EARLY (at least by 2.00 PM). Additionally, if you're in Du Gia on a WEEKEND, expect this waterfall to be CROWDED with locals by midday. Safe travels, Mega.

reviews.author: Giang Pham
reviews.text: Waaaaaay too crowded. I do not recommend unless you’d like to swim in other people’s sweat and piss lmao

reviews.author: Alex D
reviews.text: A bit hard to find where to stop riding & start walking, but if you follow Google Maps and go too far, chances are the local kids in the road will stop you and ask if you’re looking for a waterfall :) Then, one or more of them will volunteer to take you there (for a tip, if you want to thank them). A pleasant walk through the fields on the way, very lush and peaceful, surrounded by giant rocks all around. It only takes about 10 min riding from the village (due to a rough road part of the way) and then 5-10 min walking.

reviews.author: CD Le
reviews.text: From deep valley sided with ladder rice paddies to hike with constantly changing scenic views. Breathtaking untouched nature.The destination is great but the journey there is no match.

reviews.author: Srishti Shetty
reviews.text: GET HERE EARLY to truly enjoy. Got here around 8 am and it was fairly empty with few people around. As we were leaving around 9 am we came across large tour groups with their speakers.

reviews.author: Wander
reviews.text: When i went there were 3 different tour groups there with around 30 people total. No one was swimming and only people jumping. I managed to split off from my group and went up the hill. Its hard to find but there's actually another small waterfall area higher up. No one was there and I got to swim in a small cave area by myself.

reviews.author: Eva Meglič
reviews.text: Definitely a worthy stop on the Ha Giang loop. The waterfall is close to the village, but the road to there is a bit rough however totally worth it. It’s cool that they also rent towels there if you don’t have one with you. I do recommended going around 12 as the tour groups leave by then so you will have the waterfall to yourself.

reviews.author: William V
reviews.text: Do not follow the directions on Google Maps or Maps.me. In the town, near all the restaurants, there is a street to turn down, which is a paved road all the way to the parking lot for the falls. The directions on Google Maps bring you down a washed out road. The falls themselves are beautiful, and there are places nearby to stop for a drink. Definitely bring a swimsuit and a towel to take a dip!

reviews.author: Anthony Miceli
reviews.text: A bit slippery and crowded but cool place to do cliff jumping from 3 different height levels if you desire.

reviews.author: Concetta Arnao
reviews.text: One of the worst waterfalls I have ever been too. LADIES BE VERY CAREFUL there's a bunch of local men taking photos and videos of women in bikinis when you're not looking !!!!! And children so please please look after them and yourself!!!! It is really not worth it It is jammed crowded with people and shops with loud music like some sort of club. Our tour loop took us there and none of us liked it, we wish we stayed at the homestay by the pool.

reviews.author: Vy Kerekes (Cecilia)
reviews.text: The parking is quite spacious, trail is short and easy to walk, water is clean and clear; however, way too many people made noise plus loud music that completely destroyed the vibes of nature that I believe not many folks come there to search for a party

reviews.author: Tien Nguyen
reviews.text: Water super clean with nice color. You can swim here. There is a problem with the road go to here. It very hard, small, you need a good skill and carefully. Gps some time not work but there is some direction board will help you
